mnemonics: SHARE: Static Linking Hard, but Runtime Linking Easy
keyPoints: Shared libraries allow code to be loaded at runtime, reducing executable size and enabling updates without recompiling dependent programs.
They are created with the -shared and -fPIC flags and managed using environment variables like LD_LIBRARY_PATH.
The dynamic linker (ld.so) resolves symbols at load time or runtime, using tools like ldd and ldconfig for management.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to export the LD_LIBRARY_PATH variable, causing 'library not found' errors.
Linking with the shared library at compile time (-l flag) but not ensuring it's available at runtime.
Using incompatible compiler flags, like omitting -fPIC (Position Independent Code) when building the library.
